title: Create a Simple Routine
summary: Build a short daily or weekly routine for a common part of your day.
learnerPromise: You will be able to design and write down a short, repeatable routine for a part of your day that often feels chaotic.
whyItMatters: Routines reduce the number of decisions you have to make and create a sense of predictability. A simple routine can make mornings, bedtime, or after-school hours feel calmer and more manageable.
realLifeUses: Create a morning routine that gets you out the door on time
Build a bedtime routine that helps you wind down and remember essentials
Design an after-school routine for homework, snacks, and downtime
Set up a weekly routine for laundry, cleaning, or planning the week ahead
materialsNeeded: Paper and pen, or a notes app
result: A written routine with 3 to 6 steps in a logical order.
youWillLearn: How to choose one part of the day to focus on
How to list the essential tasks in the right order
How to estimate realistic time for each step
How to write the routine in a place where you will see it
miniChallenge: Write a simple 4-step routine for one part of your day and try it once today or tomorrow.
steps: Pick one part of your day that often feels rushed or disorganized.
List the 3 to 6 most important things that need to happen during that time.
Put those items in the order they should happen.
Add a rough time estimate next to each step if that helps.
Write the final routine somewhere visible and try it out.
completionCriteria: You have a written routine with clear steps in order
The routine covers a specific time of day
You have tried the routine at least once
commonProblems: Making the routine too ambitious with too many steps
Not accounting for transition time between activities
Giving up after one try instead of adjusting and repeating
tips: Start with just 3 steps and add more only after the routine feels natural
Attach your routine to something you already do, like right after breakfast
Adjust the timing after trying it once, most routines need a little tweaking
